title: pimpinella saxifraga subsp. alpina
intro: Pimpinella saxifraga subsp. alpina, also known as Alpine Burnet Saxifrage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antacid. Herbal preparations of Pimpinella saxifraga subsp. alpina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
